summary: Produced comprehensive Content Library v5 spec covering taxonomy (types/subtypes), frontmatter schema per type, merge semantics (managed vs curated fields), stable ID strategy, and directory layout. Spec is implementation-ready.

decisions.decision: Keep existing top-level types (article, link, snippet, social-post, etc.) in content_type column. Add new subtype column for finer classification within types.
decisions.rationale: Minimizes schema disruption, preserves existing queries. Subtype is optional; default null means 'generic' within type.

decisions.decision: Generate stable IDs via deterministic slug: slugify(type + '-' + subtype? + '-' + title_or_url_slug). Fallback to UUID for edge cases. IDs are immutable once created.
decisions.rationale: Deterministic IDs enable dedupe, prevent duplicates on re-ingest. slug + collision suffix (_2, _3) handles rare collisions.

decisions.decision: DB is the canonical index; filesystem files are canonical artifacts. DB.source_file_path points to the artifact. Sync reconciles both directions.
decisions.rationale: Files contain rich content/context humans may edit; DB is queryable index. Neither alone is sufficient.

decisions.decision: Three field categories: (1) managed (automation-owned, always overwritten), (2) curated (human-owned, never overwritten if non-empty), (3) derived (computed, refreshed on sync). Automation only fills missing curated fields.
decisions.rationale: Protects human curation while allowing automation to set sensible defaults.

decisions.decision: Calendly links are subtype 'scheduling-link' under type 'link'. Sync creates both file and DB record. DB stores uuid as external_id for idempotent updates.
decisions.rationale: Unified into content library taxonomy. external_id enables reliable sync without duplicate creation.

article.description: Long-form written content (saved webpages, essays, newsletters)

link.description: URL-centric resources (scheduling links, profiles, tools, references)

curated.description: Context for when to use this item

managed.description: Automation-owned fields. Always written/updated by automation.

curated.description: Human-owned contextual fields. Never overwritten if non-empty.
curated.behavior: Only fill if empty; never overwrite existing value

derived.behavior: Recompute on each sync from content/metadata

merge_algorithm: 1. Load existing item (from file frontmatter or DB record)
2. For each field in incoming data:
- If field is managed: overwrite unconditionally
- If field is curated: only write if existing value is null/empty
- If field is derived: recompute from content
4. Write back to file and/or DB

structure.articles/: Long-form content. Subdirs by topic optional (e.g., articles/vrijen/)
structure.links/: URL resources. Subdirs by source (e.g., links/calendly/)
structure.social-posts/: Social content. Subdirs by platform (e.g., social-posts/linkedin/)

rules: 2. For links: extract slug from URL path if possible
3. For Calendly: use event slug from API

invariants.name: File-DB consistency
invariants.rule: Every DB item with source_file_path must have that file exist; every file in canonical dirs should have a DB record

invariants.name: No raw scrape
invariants.rule: Body content must be clean text only (no nav, boilerplate, scripts). Ingest normalizes before storing.

invariants.name: Curated preservation
invariants.rule: Automation never overwrites non-empty curated fields
invariants.enforcement: Merge logic checks existing values before write

invariants.name: Stable IDs
invariants.rule: Item IDs are deterministic and immutable
invariants.enforcement: ID generated on first ingest, stored in frontmatter as item_id

invariants.name: Sync idempotence
invariants.rule: Running sync twice produces zero changes on second run
invariants.enforcement: Sync only writes if actual change detected

test_cases.name: Article ingest (save_webpage output)
test_cases.expected: Clean article text in body, summary in frontmatter, no nav garbage

test_cases.name: Calendly sync creates file + DB
test_cases.expected: File at links/calendly/{slug}.md AND DB record with matching id

test_cases.name: Merge preserves curated fields
test_cases.input: Existing file with audience='investors'; re-sync with automation default

test_cases.name: Merge fills missing curated
test_cases.input: Existing file with no priority; re-sync

test_cases.name: Stable ID collision
test_cases.input: Two items that would generate same slug

open_questions.question: Should summary be auto-generated via LLM on ingest, or should we defer to manual/batch?
open_questions.recommendation: Auto-generate short summary on ingest for new items without summary. Use simple extraction heuristic first, LLM fallback.

open_questions.question: How to handle items that exist in DB but not in filesystem (orphans)?
open_questions.recommendation: Sync command flags orphans. User can choose to delete DB record or recreate file.

open_questions.question: Should existing json_migration items be re-ID'd to new stable ID scheme?
open_questions.recommendation: Yes, during migration. Map old_id -> new_id in migration log for any callers.

notes_for_orchestrator: Spec is complete and implementation-ready. Key decisions: (1) subtype as new column, (2) three-category merge semantics (managed/curated/derived), (3) stable IDs via deterministic slugs, (4) DB is index, files are artifacts. W1.2 (DB schema) and W1.3 (ingest normalization) can proceed in parallel. W1.4 (Calendly sync) depends on W1.2 for schema. Migration worker (W2.2) will need the old_id -> new_id mapping strategy defined here.
